title: podman-remote build picks wrong seccomp.json file path
body:
### Issue Description

When host uses /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json and /etc/containers/seccomp.json does not exist, but podman-remote client machine has /etc/containers/seccomp.json, it'll attempt to use load /etc/containers/seccomp.json from host and fail.

```
STEP 5/6: RUN pip install .
Error: building at STEP "RUN pip install .": opening seccomp profile failed: open /etc/containers/seccomp.json: no such file or directory
```

I traced it to the following logic, where it just checks local filesystem instead of relying on what `podman-remote info` says https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2e6f29a2dfcbf1a0d191a5a50e6489edf723ceef/vendor/go.podman.io/buildah/pkg/parse/parse.go#L330-L345

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Ensure host machine has just /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
2. Ensure client machine has /etc/containers/seccomp.json
3. Run podman-remote build ...


### Describe the results you received

```
STEP 5/6: RUN pip install .
Error: building at STEP "RUN pip install .": opening seccomp profile failed: open /etc/containers/seccomp.json: no such file or directory
```

### Describe the results you expected

Image builds correctly

### podman info output

Just the relevant part::

```yaml
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: true
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

n/a

### Additional information

n/a
